examples: they cheered the rise of the hot-air balloon

meanings.definition: organized opposition to authority; a conflict in which one faction tries to wrest control from another

examples: The smoke arose from the forest fire
The mist uprose from the meadows

meanings.definition: increase in value or to a higher point
examples: the value of our house rose sharply last year
The audience got up and applauded

meanings.definition: come into existence; take on form or shape
examples: A new religious movement originated in that country
a love that sprang up from friendship
the idea for the book grew out of a short story

meanings.definition: move to a better position in life or to a better job; "She ascended from a life of poverty to one of great

examples: Sales were climbing after prices were lowered

meanings.definition: get up and out of bed
examples: I get up at 7 A.M. every day
Her new novel jumped high on the bestseller list
Her spirits rose when she heard the good news

meanings.definition: exert oneself to meet a challenge
take part in a rebellion; renounce a former allegiance

examples: the dough rose slowly in the warm room
The sun uprising sees the dusk night fled...

meanings.definition: advancing or becoming higher or greater in degree or value or status
